channel.title: Ninad Pathak
channel.description: Programmer-turned-writer helping DevTools and SaaS companies create technical content their engineering teams actually approve.

item.title: Why Your Agent Remembers the Wrong Thing: Memory Attribution Failures
item.description: Memory attribution failures cause AI agents to act on outdated or misassigned context. Here is what I found examining the failure patterns.

item.title: Building a Customer Support Agent with Persistent Memory: A Worked Example
item.description: I built a customer support agent that actually remembers across sessions. Here is what I learned about memory architecture, serialization trade-offs, and the failure modes that will kill your deployment.

item.title: LLM Inference Optimization: What Actually Works in Production
item.description: A practical breakdown of the inference optimization techniques that move the needle — batching, quantization, caching, and attention kernels — with concrete numbers and the tradeoffs between them.

item.title: Why Your Coding Agent Keeps Forgetting Everything: Memory Persistence in AI Coding Assistants
item.description: The memory persistence patterns that actually work for AI coding assistants, and why most agents lose context between sessions.

item.title: Memory Versioning and Audit Trails for Regulated AI Agents
item.description: If your agent overwrites its memory, you cannot pass a compliance audit. How to build append-only memory versioning and trace agent reasoning.

item.title: Contextual Compression for Agent Memory: What Stays and What Goes
item.description: How agents decide what to keep in memory when context space is finite, and the three compression strategies that actually work.

item.title: Episodic, Semantic, and Working Memory in AI Agents: A Practical Map
item.description: AI agents juggle three distinct memory types. Getting them wrong is the source of most agent memory failures I see in production.

item.title: Memory Serialization: How Agents Persist State Across Sessions
item.description: Why agents forget everything on restart, and the serialization patterns that actually solve it

item.title: The HERMES.md Bug That Silently Burned $200 in Claude Code Credits
item.description: A case-sensitive string in git commit messages routes Claude Code API requests through extra usage billing instead of plan quota. Here is how it works and what it means for teams.

item.title: The Memory Hierarchy: Why RAG Alone Is Not Enough for Agent Memory
item.description: RAG handles document retrieval well. It handles agent memory poorly, because agents need episodic recall, working context, and cross-session persistence that a vector store cannot provide.

item.title: Why Agent Memory Retrieval Is Asymmetric and Why It Breaks Your RAG Pipeline
item.description: The retrieval patterns that work for a query do not work when the agent is the one retrieving, and this asymmetry silently breaks production RAG systems.

item.title: Multi-Agent vs Single-Agent Systems: The Real Trade-offs
item.description: The decision between one agent and many is not about capability. It is about failure modes, latency, and operational complexity.

item.title: When to Build an Agent and When to Build a Smarter Assistant
item.description: The difference between an AI agent and a smart assistant comes down to one thing: who drives the loop.

item.title: The Agent Design Space: A Map of What Engineers Are Actually Building
item.description: After surveying production agents across industries, the design space clusters into patterns. Here is what I found.

item.title: Lambda Calculus as AI Reasoning Benchmark
item.description: I have used lambda calculus to test whether AI systems can actually reason through composition, or whether they are just pattern-matching their way to plausible outputs.

item.title: Matz's Ruby AOT Compiler: How Spinel Differs from YJIT/MJIT and What It Means for Production Ruby
item.description: Spinel compiles Ruby to standalone native binaries with 11.6x speedups over miniruby. I dug into the architecture to understand why AOT beats JIT for long-running services.

item.title: Apple's iCloud Keychain Escrow Security: How It Works and Why It Matters
item.description: Apple's iCloud Keychain escrow uses a key-splitting architecture that makes escrow records cryptographically inaccessible without the device passcode. This is how it actually works.

item.title: The Anatomy of an Agent Loop: Perceive, Think, Act, Remember
item.description: The agent loop is not one thing. It is four distinct phases that run in sequence, and understanding each one is how you debug what breaks.

item.title: Why AI Agents Keep Failing in Production and What the Field Is Doing About It
item.description: I have spent two years watching agents fail in production. Here is what I keep seeing and what the field is starting to do about it.

item.title: Python model.predict(): The Function That Turns Data Into Decisions
item.description: A practical guide to model.predict() across scikit-learn, Keras, PyTorch, and XGBoost—what it does, how it behaves differently across frameworks, and the gotchas that will bite you in production.
